steps.expected: Login page displayed in light mode
steps.observed: Login page displayed in light mode — no dark class on html, body bg rgb(250,250,250), text rgb(23,23,23)
Redirected to http://localhost:3002/hub — Portal Hub page displayed with theme toggle visible

steps.action: Verify light mode state before toggle
steps.expected: html has no dark class, light background and dark text
steps.observed: html.className='', dark class absent, body bg rgb(250,250,250), text color rgb(23,23,23)

steps.action: Click theme toggle button ('Switch to dark mode')
steps.expected: html gains dark class, colors switch to dark theme, text remains readable
steps.observed: html.className='dark', body bg switched to rgb(17,17,17), text color switched to rgb(250,250,250) — dark theme active, text clearly readable

evidence.network: n/a — client-side only toggle, no API calls

steps.action: Verify dark mode is active after VAL-FOUNDATION-006 toggle
Verify dark theme persists after reload
steps.expected: html still has dark class, dark background/light text maintained, localStorage.o-theme still 'dark'
steps.observed: html.className='dark', localStorage.o-theme='dark', body bg rgb(17,17,17), text rgb(250,250,250) — dark theme fully persisted via localStorage

frictions.description: Theme toggle is not available on the login page — it only appears on authenticated pages (Portal Hub, portal layouts). Had to log in first to test the toggle.
frictions.resolution: Logged in with admin credentials to access the Portal Hub page where the theme toggle is located

summary: Tested 2 assertions: 2 passed. Dark mode toggle correctly adds 'dark' class to html element, switches colors (background rgb(250,250,250)→rgb(17,17,17), text rgb(23,23,23)→rgb(250,250,250)), and text remains fully readable in both modes. Theme preference persists via localStorage key 'o-theme' across page reloads.
